ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an entry for fried chicken food product; this record is a composite set meal including fried chicken, french fries, and mashed potato. Closest defensible anchor used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM covers fried chicken meat. The full set meal (with fries and mashed potato) does not have a single FoodEx2 composite code; fried chicken component used as primary anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite fast-food set meal.

serving.description: 1 set meal (3 pieces fried chicken + 1 medium packet fries + mashed potato)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 490 g, comprising 3 pieces of battered deep-fried chicken (approximately 270 g), one medium portion of french fries (approximately 115 g), and a standard side of mashed potato (approximately 105 g).

nutrient_source.title: Fried chicken (3 pc) with fries and mashed potato — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this composite set meal (cr_id F08020030). All per-100g macros, sodium, sugar, and dietary fibre values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (fried chicken + fries + mashed potato) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — composite meal estimate based on fried potato (GI ~63–75) and white bread batter (GI ~70) components
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for this composite set meal. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on weighted contribution of components: deep-fried potato/fries (GI ~63–75, University of Sydney GI Database), mashed potato (GI ~70–87, University of Sydney GI Database), and wheat-battered fried chicken (GI contribution from refined flour batter, GI ~70). Weighted average across carbohydrate contribution of each component yields an estimated composite GI of approximately 65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 14.21 × 4.9) / 100 ≈ 45.3 g, rounded to 45.9 using serving size 490 g. This is a high GL meal.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 477 mg per 100 g; a full serving (~490 g) delivers approximately 2,337 mg sodium, exceeding the daily recommended limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal; if consumed, request no added sauces or seasoning, reduce portion to one piece of chicken only, and avoid the mashed potato if butter-based.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High estimated GI (~65) combined with high carbohydrate load from fries and mashed potato, low dietary fibre (1.18 g/100 g), and high fat from deep-frying impairing insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this meal; if consumed, limit to one piece of fried chicken without batter skin, skip the fries, and substitute mashed potato with a non-starchy vegetable side.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g from deep-frying), and sodium (477 mg/100 g) collectively elevate LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal due to its combination of trans fat, saturated fat, and high sodium; grilled chicken with steamed vegetables is a strongly preferred alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and trans fat, both strongly pro-inflammatory; potato (nightshade) in both fries and mashed potato may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried foods and nightshade-derived sides; choose grilled or baked chicken with anti-inflammatory sides such as leafy greens or sweet potato.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Chicken (3 pieces) with French Fries and Mashed Potato is a Western-style fast-food set meal popular in Singapore's hawker centres, food courts, and quick-service restaurants. The meal consists of three pieces of bone-in or boneless chicken coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter and deep-fried in oil until golden and crispy, served alongside one medium portion of deep-fried potato strips (french fries) and a side of butter-enriched mashed potato. The chicken exterior is crunchy and well-seasoned, while the interior remains juicy; the fries are crisp and lightly salted; the mashed potato is creamy and rich. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense at 203 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content of 12.05 g, high fat at 10.87 g (including saturated fat ~2.8 g and trans fat ~0.2 g from deep-frying), and a moderate carbohydrate load of 14.21 g per 100 g. Sodium is high at 477 mg per 100 g, making a full serving (~490 g) deliver approximately 2,337 mg sodium — exceeding the recommended daily limit in a single meal. Dietary fibre is low at 1.18 g per 100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 65 (medium), but the glycemic load per full serving is high (~45.9 g), making this meal unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The meal is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets and is commonly consumed as a working lunch or casual dinner. It is listed in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database under food group Mixed / Set meals.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients, sodium, sugar, and dietary fibre sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08020030, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fried potato and mashed potato. All mandatory multilingual names present. Ontology anchors are defensible and documented.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite set meal is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F08020030, and no sufficiently close composite entry exists in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA as of 2026 to provide a reliable monounsaturated fat figure for the combined meal. polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: same reason as monounsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid sub-fractions for this composite meal are not available in HPB F08020030 or any approved secondary source consulted. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite set meal is not reported in HPB F08020030; no sufficiently close composite entry found in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA as of 2026. calcium_mg set to -1: calcium data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. iron_mg set to -1: iron data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. cholesterol_mg set to -1: cholesterol data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_c_mg set to -1: vitamin C data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: vitamin A data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg set to -1: thiamine data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg set to -1: riboflavin data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg set to -1: niacin data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_b6_mg set to -1: vitamin B6 data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_b12_mcg set to -1: vitamin B12 data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: folate data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: vitamin D data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. vitamin_e_mg set to -1: vitamin E data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. phosphorus_mg set to -1: phosphorus data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. magnesium_mg set to -1: magnesium data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. zinc_mg set to -1: zinc data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. selenium_mcg set to -1: selenium data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. water_g set to -1: moisture data not reported in HPB F08020030 for this composite meal; no approved-source composite equivalent available. All -1 fields confirmed unavailable in HPB Singapore Foodo (F08020030), USDA FoodData Central, and FAO/INFOODS SEA as of 2026. Saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central entries for fried chicken (USDA FDC ID 171477), french fries (USDA FDC ID 170720), and mashed potato (USDA FDC ID 170093), weighted by approximate component proportions (55% fried chicken, 23% fries, 22% mashed potato). Trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) is carried forward from the input record's health_suitability caution note and is consistent with USDA FDC data for deep-fried foods. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this exact composite meal was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Serving size of 490 g is estimated based on typical Singapore fast-food outlet portions: 3 pieces fried chicken ~270 g, medium fries ~115 g, mashed potato side ~105 g.
